questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital and largest city of France.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet due to iron oxide on its surface.

questions.question: What is the largest mammal in the world?
questions.explanation: The blue whale is the largest mammal, reaching lengths of up to 100 feet.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote Romeo and Juliet in the late 16th century.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol for gold is Au, from its Latin name 'aurum'.

questions.question: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: There are 7 continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania, and South America.

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamond is the hardest natural substance on Earth, scoring 10 on the Mohs scale.

questions.question: Which ocean is the largest?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean, covering more than 60 million square miles.

questions.question: What gas do plants absorb from the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere during photosynthesis.

questions.question: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa in the early 16th century.

questions.question: What is the capital of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the capital and largest city of Japan.

questions.question: What is the main ingredient in traditional Japanese miso soup?
questions.explanation: Miso soup is made from fermented soybeans, called miso.

questions.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, with an average distance of about 36 million miles.

questions.question: What is the smallest prime number?
questions.explanation: The smallest prime number is 2, which is also the only even prime number.

questions.question: Who discovered penicillin?
questions.explanation: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928.

questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra is the capital of Australia, selected as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.question: What is the main language spoken in Brazil?
questions.explanation: Portuguese is the official language of Brazil, due to its colonial history.

questions.question: Which scientist developed the theory of relativity?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity in the early 20th century.

questions.question: What is the largest organ in the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ in the human body, covering about 20 square feet.

questions.question: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: The currency of the United Kingdom is the Pound Sterling (£).

questions.question: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon has six sides, as indicated by the prefix 'hexa-' meaning six.

questions.question: What is the boiling point of water at sea level?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100°C (212°F) at standard atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Neil Armstrong was the first person to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969.

questions.question: What is the tallest mammal?
questions.explanation: The giraffe is the tallest mammal, reaching heights of up to 18 feet.

questions.question: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa is the capital of Canada, located in the province of Ontario.

questions.question: Which gas makes up the majority of Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere.

questions.question: What is the largest desert in the world?
questions.explanation: The Antarctic Desert is the largest desert in the world, covering about 5.5 million square miles.

questions.question: Who wrote the novel '1984'?
questions.explanation: George Orwell wrote the dystopian novel '1984' in 1949.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula for water?
questions.explanation: The chemical formula for water is H2O, meaning two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

questions.question: What is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, capable of reaching speeds up to 70 mph.

questions.question: Which country is known as the Land of the Rising Sun?
questions.explanation: Japan is known as the Land of the Rising Sun due to its eastern position relative to China.

questions.question: What is the main component of natural gas?
questions.explanation: Methane (CH4) is the main component of natural gas.

questions.question: Who painted the Starry Night?
questions.explanation: Vincent van Gogh painted The Starry Night in 1889.

questions.question: What is the capital of India?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the capital of India.

questions.question: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: The adult human body has 206 bones.

questions.question: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.

questions.question: Who invented the telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with inventing the telephone in 1876.

questions.question: What is the pH level of pure water?
questions.explanation: Pure water has a neutral pH of 7.

questions.question: Which element has the chemical symbol 'Fe'?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol 'Fe' stands for iron, from its Latin name 'ferrum'.

questions.question: What is the main language spoken in Egypt?
questions.explanation: Arabic is the official language of Egypt.

questions.question: What is the capital of Russia?
questions.explanation: Moscow is the capital of Russia.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced when skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is produced when skin is exposed to sunlight.

questions.question: What is the largest island in the world?
questions.explanation: Greenland is the largest island in the world.

questions.question: Who developed the theory of evolution by natural selection?
questions.explanation: Charles Darwin developed the theory of evolution by natural selection.

questions.question: What is the hardest substance known to man?
questions.explanation: While diamond is hard, graphene is actually harder in terms of tensile strength.

questions.question: What is the capital of Brazil?
questions.explanation: Brasília is the capital of Brazil, purpose-built in 1960.

questions.question: Which planet has the most moons?
questions.explanation: Saturn has the most moons in our solar system, with over 80 confirmed moons.

questions.question: What is the main ingredient in hummus?
questions.explanation: The main ingredient in hummus is chickpeas.

questions.question: Who was the first female prime minister of the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Margaret Thatcher was the first female prime minister of the United Kingdom, serving from 1979 to 1990.

questions.question: What is the smallest bone in the human body?
questions.explanation: The stapes, located in the middle ear, is the smallest bone in the human body.
